title: Automatic Data Processing Is Simply Too Expensive
text: Automatic Data Processing is a blue-chip software company with almost 50 years of dividend growth. The company has shown steady growth in sales and EPS, with room for further expansion. However, there are risks of competition from larger software companies and a lack of margin of safety in the current valuation.

title: S&P 500 Gains and Losses Today: Stocks Mixed After Fed Lifts Rates
text: The S&P 500 was little changed on Wednesday, July 26, 2023 after the Federal Reserve raised interest rates to fight inflation.

title: Automatic Data Processing (ADP) Q4 Earnings Beat, Rise Y/Y
text: Automatic Data Processing's (ADP) revenues grow year over year.

title: Is Automatic Data Processing (ADP) Stock Fairly Valued? An In-Depth Analysis
text: With a daily gain of 6.1% and an Earnings Per Share (EPS) (EPS) of $7.83, Automatic Data Processing Inc ( ADP , Financial) presents an intriguing investment opportunity. This article aims to answer a crucial question: Is ADP's stock fairly valued?

title: Compared to Estimates, ADP (ADP) Q4 Earnings: A Look at Key Metrics
text: Although the revenue and EPS for ADP (ADP) give a sense of how its business performed in the quarter ended June 2023, it might be worth considering how some key metrics compare with Wall Street estimates and the year-ago numbers.

title: Automatic Data Processing (ADP) Beats Q4 Earnings and Revenue Estimates
text: Automatic Data Processing (ADP) came out with quarterly earnings of $1.89 per share, beating the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $1.83 per share. This compares to earnings of $1.50 per share a year ago.

title: Toast Sets New Standard in Restaurant Management Platforms
text: At first glance, it's easy to mistake Toast, Inc. NASDAQ: TOST as a delivery application like Door Dash Inc. NASDAQ: DASH or an Uber Technologies Inc. NASDAQ: UBER Uber Eats service when placing a restaurant online order. However, it's a complete restaurant ecosystem management platform.

title: ADP Reports Fourth Quarter and Fiscal 2023 Results
text: ROSELAND, N.J. , July 26, 2023  /PRNewswire/ -- ADP (Nasdaq: ADP), a leading global technology company providing human capital management (HCM) solutions, today announced its fourth quarter and fiscal 2023 financial results along with its fiscal 2024 outlook through an earnings release available on the company's website at investors.adp.com/events-and-presentations.

title: Our Top 10 Picks For Dividend Growth Stocks - July 2023
text: This monthly series of articles focuses on DGI stocks that are likely to provide a high rate of dividend growth rather than a high current yield. We use our proprietary models to rate quantitatively and qualitatively and select the top ten names from an initial list of nearly 400 dividend stocks. A balanced portfolio should have a mix of high-dividend growth stocks and high-yield dividend stocks, depending on the life stage. If you are in the accumulation phase and are several years away from retirement, high-growth dividend stocks would be more appropriate for long-term growth.

title: What's in the Offing for Automatic Data's (ADP) Q4 Earnings?
text: Automatic Data's (ADP) earnings are likely to increase year over year on the back of improving segmental performance.

title: With The Massive NASDAQ Rebalance Coming Soon 7 ‘Strong Buy' Stocks Could Explode Higher
text: While the market rally this year has been an investor's dream after the nightmare of last year, the reality is that a handful of red-hot technology stocks running higher on the back of the Artificial Intelligence explosion has created almost all of the gains in the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq.

title: CPI Comes in Lower Than Expected
text: Soft-landing, anyone? This morning's much-anticipated Consumer Price Index (CPI) report for June were below expectations across the board — and these days, that's definitely good news.

title: June CPI: The Fed Winning the War on Inflation?
text: The "Inflation Rate" came down 100 basis points month over month to +3.0% from the +4.0% reported a month ago.

title: The Ultimate Buffett-Style 5% Yielding Dividend Aristocrat Portfolio
text: The economic data is showing that a recession is almost certainly coming, but it now appears to be most likely to start in the first half of 2024. Impatience, and fear of missing out, have caused many investors to forget the painful lessons of 2022, chasing momentum despite insane valuations in some stocks. Wall Street isn't a game won by the smartest but by the most prudent and patient.

title: Wall Street Awaits CPI, PPI Inflation Data
text: We're still more than two weeks away from the next Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting, which will determine the course of interest rate levels through the remainder of the summer, but the Fed remains almost magnet-like in its ability to pull interest toward it this far out. This week brings us the Consumer Price Index (CPI), Producer Price Index (PPI) and the start of Q2 earnings season — all of which carry to potential to augment the Fed's next decision.

title: 4 Factor Dividend Growth Portfolio - Cruising Towards A 20% Return
text: The 4-factor dividend growth portfolio is a strategy that leverages the stock selection process of Schwab U.S. Dividend Equity ETF™, with a few minor twists. The portfolio gained 5.00% in June, underperforming the S&P 500 by 1.60%. Year-to-date, the portfolio is up 11.99% and trailing the S&P 500 by 4.90%. Since inception, the portfolio is generating 3.56% of alpha over the S&P 500.

title: 5 Relatively Safe And Cheap Dividend Stocks To Invest In - July 2023
text: This article is part of our monthly series where we highlight five companies that are large-cap, relatively safe, dividend-paying, and are offering large discounts to their historical norms. We go over our filtering process to select just five conservative DGI stocks from more than 7,500 companies that are traded on U.S. exchanges, including OTC networks. In addition to the primary list that yields just under 3%, we present two other groups of five DGI stocks each, with the goal of moderate to high yields.
